seo.title: Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents in Mastra Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix sensitive data leakage prevention in ai agents in Mastra agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Mastra agents, sensitive data leakage often occurs when workflow step outputs containing PII or secrets are passed unvalidated between transitions, exposing data in logs or downstream tools. Agent tool calls can return malformed responses with embedded credentials due to poor input sanitization, while memory retrieval injects stale context carrying sensitive historical data into current sessions. Sync integrations exacerbate this by pulling unfiltered external data, causing agents to inadvertently leak it in responses.

howItManifests.scenario: Workflow step outputs not validated between transitions
howItManifests.symptom: PII from user profiles appears in subsequent workflow logs
howItManifests.impact: Compliance violations and data exposure in audit trails

howItManifests.scenario: Agent tool calls returning malformed data
howItManifests.symptom: API keys visible in tool response payloads
howItManifests.impact: Credentials compromised through agent conversation history

howItManifests.symptom: Old credit card numbers appear in current conversation context
howItManifests.impact: Historical sensitive data contaminates fresh interactions

howItManifests.scenario: Sync integration data causing agent confusion
howItManifests.symptom: Customer database records leaked in agent responses
howItManifests.impact: Massive data exposure through third-party sync failures

detection.name: Workflow Output PII Scanner
detection.description: Intercepts Mastra workflow step outputs and scans for common PII patterns using regex

detection.name: Tool Response Validator
detection.description: Validates Mastra agent tool call responses for embedded secrets and API keys

detection.name: Memory Context Auditor
detection.description: Audits Mastra memory retrievals for stale sensitive data injection

fixes.name: Workflow Output Sanitizer Middleware
fixes.description: Mastra workflow middleware that redacts PII before step transitions

fixes.name: Safe Tool Response Wrapper
fixes.description: Wraps Mastra agent tools to sanitize responses before returning to LLM

fixes.name: Filtered Memory Retrieval
fixes.description: Mastra memory hook that filters sensitive data from retrieved context

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly as middleware in the Mastra agent pipeline, automatically scanning all workflow transitions, tool responses, memory retrievals, and sync integrations for PII and secrets. Using real-time regex pattern matching and ML-based entity recognition, Vex flags violations before data reaches the LLM or external systems, providing detailed remediation traces.

Vex's Mastra integration captures the full context of leakage events - including workflow step lineage, tool call parameters, and memory retrieval metadata - enabling precise root cause analysis. Automated remediation rules can instantly apply sanitization, block risky transitions, or trigger human review workflows.

The Vex SDK provides TypeScript-native hooks for Mastra's workflow, agent, and memory systems, with zero-config setup via npm install mastra @vex_dev/sdk. Detection rules are customizable and shareable across teams, ensuring consistent security posture across all Mastra deployments.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex middleware in your Mastra agents today to prevent sensitive data leakage with zero code changes
